articles.title: Partnerships & Stakeholders: Science and Technology Community
articles.description: The role of global science, academia, research and innovation is enshrined in the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction where it is recognized for the catalytic, boundary-pushing and transformative potential it holds for addressing the dynamic drivers of disaster risk.

articles.title: Morocco advances national early warning systems to protect communities from natural hazards
articles.description: Morocco advances national early warning systems to protect communities from natural hazards

articles.title: Statement on Venezuela earthquakes by Kamal Kishore, Special Representative of the UN Secretary-General for Disaster Risk Reduction
articles.description: Statement on Venezuela earthquakes by Kamal Kishore, Special Representative of the UN Secretary-General for Disaster Risk Reduction

articles.title: Save the Date: Africa Early Warnings Forum, Addis Ababa, Ethiopia 2-4 September 2026
articles.description: The Africa Early Warnings Forum will serve as an implementation and partnership platform focused on recommendations for rapidly scaling effective and sustainable multi-hazard early warning systems across Africa.

articles.title: Tajikistan to establish framework for Disaster Risk Information Exchange
articles.description: A consultative meeting on Disaster Risk Information Exchange convened in Dushanbe, brought together national specialists and international experts dedicated to DRR and the enhancement of multi-hazard early warning systems.

articles.title: Interagency working group of Tajikistan's national DRR platform agrees on approaches to monitor the implementation of the national strategy
articles.description: The Interagency Experts Working Group of the National Platform for Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) discusses strategic tools for enhancing the effectiveness of risk management, improving data collection, and developing early warning systems in the country.

articles.title: UNDRR brings local voices to the center at the 13th European Urban Resilience Forum
articles.description: The 13th EURESFO, held in Guimarães, Portugal, brought together urban resilience practitioners, city leaders, and international partners to accelerate local action on disaster risk reduction (DRR), climate adaptation, and resilient urban governance.

articles.title: Eleven lessons in risk governance from the Panama Canal
articles.description: In more than a century of operation, the authorities that manage the Panama Canal have accumulated some valuable lessons in risk governance that are relevant to other major infrastructure systems – and to disaster risk reduction more broadly.

articles.title: UNDRR announces "Resilience Starts at Home" as the theme for the 2026 International Day for Disaster Risk Reduction
articles.description: The United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction (UNDRR) today announced that the theme for the 2026 International Day for Disaster Risk Reduction (IDDRR), observed on 13 October, will be “Resilience Starts at Home.”

articles.title: Advancing urban disaster resilience through global collaboration and South-South and Triangular Cooperation
articles.description: UNDRR, UNOSSC, PAHO, and UNTB held their 6th training in Apr-May 2026. The program boosted urban disaster resilience in developing and least developed countries by leveraging technological innovation, evidence-based decisions, and South-South cooperation.

articles.title: Building a safer society through science and technology: a compendium of Korea’s science and technology-based disaster management
articles.description: This casebook highlights the implementation of science and technology-based disaster management, featuring 24 Korean practices across four thematic areas, along with Korea's success factors and remaining challenges. It is available in English and Korean.

articles.title: The value of loss and damage data, from early warning to recovery
articles.description: From drought preparedness in Africa to institutional strengthening in Kyrgyzstan and post-conflict recovery planning in the agricultural sector in Lebanon, explore how loss and damage data is being used as a resource for decision-making.

articles.title: Tajikistan reviews progress on national early warning systems roadmap
articles.description: Tajikistan National Pillar Leads convened the second coordination meeting with relevant state agencies and international partners to review the implementation of the National Roadmap on Enhancing Early Warning Systems for 2024–2027.
